steps.title: Calculate labor costs for total hours
steps.content:
The company charges $112 for the first hour and $40 for each additional hour. For $x > 1$, total labor cost is:

$$112 + 40(x - 1)$$

Simplify by distributing and combining constants: $$112 + 40x - 40 = 40x + 72$$

steps.title: Add the cost of parts to the total
steps.content:
The total cost $y$ includes the labor cost $40x + 72$ plus the parts cost $z$:

$$y = 40x + 72 + z$$

Rearranged, this becomes $$y = 40x + z + 72$$, which matches choice B — B is correct.
